message:
perf(protocol): Use Cow<'static, str> for jsonrpc version field

Replaces String with Cow<'static, str> for the jsonrpc field in
JsonRpcRequest and JsonRpcResponse. This eliminates a heap allocation
for the static "2.0" version string on every JSON-RPC message creation.

- Add JSONRPC_VERSION constant for zero-copy references
- Custom serde functions return borrowed reference for "2.0"
- All callers updated to use Cow::Borrowed(JSONRPC_VERSION)

Isomorphism: Wire format is byte-for-byte identical. Non-standard
versions (if any) are preserved via Cow::Owned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -423,7 +423,7 @@ fn create_and_initialize_client(
 
     // Send initialized notification
     let notification = JsonRpcRequest {
-        jsonrpc: "2.0".to_string(),
+        jsonrpc: std::borrow::Cow::Borrowed(fastmcp_protocol::JSONRPC_VERSION),
         method: "initialized".to_string(),
         params: Some(serde_json::json!({})),
         id: None,
